signals.headline: Alpenglow Consensus Upgrade Confirmed for Transaction Speed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Alpenglow Consensus Upgrade Confirmed for Transaction Speed | YES | HIGH confidence**
The Alpenglow upgrade passed governance voting with 98.27% approval and aims to reduce transaction finality from 12 seconds to 150 milliseconds.[1][5] This multi-year catalyst remains a key driver for SOL price models targeting $150–$260 by end of Q2 2026.[5]

signals.headline: STRAIT OF HORMUZ REMAINS EFFECTIVELY CLOSED | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ REMAINS EFFECTIVELY CLOSED | NO | HIGH confidence**

Despite Iran's commitment to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as part of the ceasefire, shipping remains suspended[2]. Danish shipping company Maersk stated the ceasefire has "yet to provide enough security" for normal operations in this critical waterway through which a fifth of global oil supplies normally pass[2].

signals.headline: Interactive Brokers Launches SOL Trading for European Investors | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Interactive Brokers Launches SOL Trading for European Investors | YES | HIGH confidence**
Interactive Brokers opened Solana trading to European traders through Zero Hash, expanding institutional access.[3] This infrastructure expansion removed a previous friction point for European institutional capital.

signals.headline: Solana Hits 167 Million Monthly Token Holders Record | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Hits 167 Million Monthly Token Holders Record | YES | HIGH confidence**
As of April 14, 2026, Solana reached an all-time record of 167 million monthly token holders with daily non-vote transactions hitting 148 million.[1] This represents significant network growth that could influence long-term price predictions.
